revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Isohama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:磯浜古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site | name =Isohama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 磯浜古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Hisagezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg; File:Hisagezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Hisagezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Ibaraki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Isohama Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|36|18|57.60|N|140|34|13.33|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}}; {{Coord|36.316|N|140.5703611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Ōarai, Ibaraki|en|Ōarai, Ibaraki|qid=Q1349185|lt=Ōarai, Ibaraki|1=Ōarai, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}; {{ill|Oarai|qid=Q1349185|1=Ōarai, Ibaraki}}| region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd - 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1949, 2011-2021, 2019-2020 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|18|57.60|N|140|34|13.33|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Isohama Kofun Cluster'''|磯浜古墳群|Isohama Kofun-gun}} is a group of ancient [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]]s, located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Ōarai, Ibaraki|en|Ōarai, Ibaraki|qid=Q1349185|lt=Ōarai, Ibaraki|1=Ōarai, Ibaraki}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Three of the tumuli at this site were designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2020 and three more are under consideration.{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/449870|title=磯浜古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 March 2021}} ==Overview== The Isohama Kofun Cluster is located in the central part of Ibaraki prefecture, facing the {{ill|Pacific Ocean|en|Pacific Ocean|qid=Q98|lt=Pacific Ocean|1=Pacific Ocean}}, about three kilometers southwest of the mouth of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]]. The site is located on a hill and consists of two {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, one "two-conjoined rectangles" type ''kofun'' ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), two {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and two of indeterminate shape. The site once contained many more tumuli, most of which were destroyed due to land development and urban encroachment. The keyhole-shaped tumuli have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], and over 4100 [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s were recovered, including two [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, iron swords, jade ''[[magatama]]'', and jasper tubes, balls and other jewelry, and facsimiles of everyday objects. A number of human remains were also found. The Hisagezuka Kofun, with a total length of 101.4 meters is one of the largest in Ibaraki Prefecture. The posterior circular portion is 65 meters in diameter and numerous ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been recovered. Likewise, the Kurumazuka Kofun, with a diameter of 88 meters is one of the largest circular-type ''kofun'' in Japan. From they grave goods and construction techniques, it is estimated that these tumuli were built from the mid-3rd century to late 4th century. The surrounding Higekama Ipponmatsu site is a large-scale settlement trace that had continued from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} into the Kofun period, so it can be assumed that this necropolis is for the local kings of this region. Its location overlooking the ocean and the mouth of the Naka River also hints at the possibility that these local kings had some maritime connection to the Yamato region of western Japan. The Ōarai Isozaki Shrine is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located nearby and is dedicated to the Nakakunizo clan, which may also have a connection to these tumuli. The site is a 12-minute walk from {{ill|Ōarai Station|en|Ōarai Station|qid=Q11437232|lt=Ōarai Station|1=Ōarai Station}}. {| class="wikitable"style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Isohama Kofun Cluster |- ! !! name !! location !! type !! Size !! Date !! Status |- | 1 || Himezuka Kofun
姫塚古墳 || {{coords|36|19|4.55|N|140|34|15.95|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark}} || co-joined rectangles || 29 m length || mid-3rd C || National H.S. |- | 2 || Gohonmatsu Kofun
五本松古墳 || {{coords|36|19|3.6|N|140|34|10.8|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark}} || indeterminate || - || late-3rd to early 4th || |- | 3 || Gohonmatsushita Kofun
五本松下古墳 || {{coords|36|19|4.8|N|140|34|12.9|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark}} || indeterminate || - || late-3rd to early 4th || |- | 4 || Bochiyama Kofun
坊主山古墳|| {{coords|36|19|1.57|N|140|34|19.48|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark}} || Keyhole || 63 m length|| early 4th C || |- | 5 || Hisagezuka Kofun
日下ヶ塚古墳|| {{coords|36|18|57.60|N|140|34|13.33|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark}} || Keyhole || 101.4 m length|| mid-4th C ||National H.S. |- | 6 || Kurumazuka Kofun
車塚古墳|| {{coords|36|19|2.55|N|140|34|34.70|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark}} || Dome || 88 m length|| late-4th C || National H.S. |} File:Himezuka Kofun (Oarai), funkyu.jpg|Himezuka Kofun File:Bochanoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Bochiyama Kofun File:Isohama Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kurumazuka Kofun File:Isohama Jinya-ato, gaikan.jpg|Site of Isohama Jin'ya ==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)|qid=Q16248240|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category:Isohama Kofun Group}} * [http://www.town.oarai.lg.jp/~syougai/syogai/info-3547-288_3.html Ōarai Town Official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.edu.pref.ibaraki.jp/board/bunkazai/kuni/shiseki/12-33/12-33.html Ibaraki Department of Education official site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Isohama Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Isohama Kofun Group}} [[ja:磯浜古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q60990014}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
